message:
fix(js): add null-safety guards in router, service worker, and perf tests

In router.js, _handleHashChange() now guards against
window.location.hash being null or undefined by wrapping it in
(window.location.hash || '') before calling .slice(1). This prevents
a TypeError in environments where the hash property may not be set.

In sw-register.js, both clearCache() and getVersion() replace
direct property access `navigator.serviceWorker.controller` with
optional chaining `navigator?.serviceWorker?.controller`. This
prevents TypeError crashes in contexts where the Service Worker API
is unavailable (e.g., insecure origins, SSR, or older browsers).

In tests/performance/run_perf.js, the Lighthouse result extraction
is hardened with optional chaining and fallback defaults (|| 0) for
all five metrics (performanceScore, fcp, lcp, tti, tbt). Previously,
if Lighthouse returned an incomplete result object (e.g., due to a
timeout or partial failure), accessing nested properties like
lhr.categories.performance.score would throw a TypeError. The
performanceScore uses a ternary with explicit null check to avoid
the edge case where score is 0 being treated as falsy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
